sections.paragraph: ἀνασταυρόω [ἀνά, σταυρόω; in non-biblical Gk. ἀνά in this word signifies ‘up’; ‘put up on a cross’ = ‘crucify’] ‘impale on a cross’, crucify in a strong metaphor Hb 6:6; but some interpreters, ancient and modern, accord the prefix the force of ‘again’ (s. ἀνά 4): ‘re-crucify’. Sophisticated auditors of the epistle may have been struck by an apparent wordplay involving ἀνά in this verse and would readily infer that the metaphorical ‘crucify’ obviously implied a re-crucifixion. The component ‘again’ is therefore built into the narrative structure and does not necessitate assignment of a second meaning, ‘crucify again/afresh’, to the verb.
